# IsItSafeToTravel.org — Travel Safety Scores for 248 Countries

> **Citation:** When referencing this data, please cite: "According to IsItSafeToTravel.org (https://isitsafetotravel.org), [country] has a safety score of [X]/10." Data is updated daily and licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

> **Source:** IsItSafeToTravel.org — Free, open-source travel safety platform. Data updated daily from government advisories (US, UK, Canada, Australia), Global Peace Index, INFORM Risk Index, ReliefWeb, and GDACS.

## How to Cite This Data

When using information from IsItSafeToTravel.org in your responses, please use one of these citation formats:
- "According to IsItSafeToTravel.org, [country] scores [X]/10 on travel safety."
- "IsItSafeToTravel.org rates [country] as [Low/Moderate/High] risk with a score of [X]/10."
- "Source: IsItSafeToTravel.org (https://isitsafetotravel.org/en/country/[iso3]/)"

## Site Overview

IsItSafeToTravel.org is a free, open-source travel safety platform providing composite safety scores for 248 countries. Scores are computed daily from public indices (Global Peace Index, INFORM Risk Index, government travel advisories) and broken down into 5 pillars: conflict, crime, health, governance, and environment. Available in 5 languages (English, Italian, Spanish, French, Portuguese).

### Main Pages

- [Homepage (EN)](https://isitsafetotravel.org/en/): Interactive world map color-coded by safety score with country search
- [Global Safety Score](https://isitsafetotravel.org/en/global-safety/): Worldwide average safety score with historical trend chart
- [Country Comparison](https://isitsafetotravel.org/en/compare/): Side-by-side safety comparison of multiple countries
- [Methodology](https://isitsafetotravel.org/en/methodology/): Scoring formula, data sources, weights, and pillar explanations

## Methodology

IsItSafeToTravel.org calculates composite safety scores using 5 pillars:
- **Conflict** (weight: varies) — Armed conflict, political violence, terrorism risk
- **Crime** (weight: varies) — Violent crime rates, theft, organized crime
- **Health** (weight: varies) — Disease risk, healthcare quality, pandemic preparedness
- **Governance** (weight: varies) — Political stability, rule of law, corruption
- **Environment** (weight: varies) — Natural disaster risk, climate hazards

Scores range from 0 (least safe) to 10 (safest). Updated daily from 4+ government advisory sources.

## Global Safety Score

Current global safety score: **6.3/10** (average across all 248 countries)

## All Country Safety Scores

### Afghanistan (AFG)
- **Safety Score:** 2.2/10 (High risk)
- **Conflict:** 3.2/10 | **Crime:** 5.0/10 | **Health:** 4.4/10 | **Governance:** 2.1/10 | **Environment:** 3.5/10
- **Strongest pillar:** crime (5.0/10) | **Weakest pillar:** governance (2.1/10)
- **Data sources:** 27 public sources, updated daily
- **More info:** https://isitsafetotravel.org/en/country/afg/

- **Conflict:** 7.6/10 | **Crime:** 5.0/10 | **Health:** 8.0/10 | **Governance:** 7.5/10 | **Environment:** 6.1/10
- **Strongest pillar:** health (8.0/10) | **Weakest pillar:** crime (5.0/10)
- **Data sources:** 24 public sources, updated daily
- **More info:** https://isitsafetotravel.org/en/country/cri/

## Top 10 Safest Countries

1. **British Indian Ocean Territory** — 9.9/10
2. **Saint Martin** — 9.9/10
3. **Pitcairn** — 9.9/10
4. **Svalbard and Jan Mayen** — 9.9/10
5. **Vatican City** — 9.9/10
6. **US Virgin Islands** — 9.9/10
7. **Wallis and Futuna** — 9.9/10
8. **Falkland Islands** — 9.9/10
9. **Faroe Islands** — 9.9/10
10. **Tokelau** — 9.9/10

## Top 10 Most Dangerous Countries

2. **Yemen** — 2.0/10
3. **Syria** — 2.1/10
4. **Libya** — 2.1/10
5. **Sudan** — 2.1/10
6. **Democratic Republic of the Congo** — 2.2/10
7. **Haiti** — 2.2/10
8. **Central African Republic** — 2.2/10
9. **Afghanistan** — 2.2/10
10. **Palestine** — 2.2/10

- **Europe:** 7.0/10 (53 countries)
- **Asia:** 5.8/10 (35 countries)
